Just now

--

(Syndicated from the original post on en.sotool.top/blog. Read it there for the latest updates.)
Processing a single PDF is easy. Processing ten? Fifty? Hundreds? That is where most people give up — or pay for a desktop tool they barely use.
This guide shows how to batch-process PDFs directly in your browser using en.sotool.top/batch-merge, en.sotool.top/batch-compress, and more. No installation, no signup, and your files never leave your device.
What batch processing solves
Imagine you have:

• 20 scanned invoices that all need the same watermark

• 15 contracts that need to be merged into one master file

• 50 PDFs that are too large for email

• 100 documents that need page numbers added
Doing these one by one takes hours. Doing them in batch takes minutes.
The batch tools available
1. Batch Merge
Combine multiple PDFs into one file. Upload all your documents at once, the tool merges them in order, and you download a single ZIP with the merged result.
en.sotool.top/batch-merge
2. Batch Compress
Reduce file sizes for dozens of PDFs simultaneously. Each file is compressed independently with the same settings, and all results are packaged into one ZIP.
en.sotool.top/batch-compress
3. Batch Rotate
Rotate pages across multiple PDFs. Need to fix the orientation of 30 scanned documents? Select the angle (90°, 180°, or 270°) and process them all at once.
en.sotool.top/batch-rotate
4. Batch Watermark
Add the same text watermark to hundreds of PDFs. Perfect for marking documents as “Draft,” “Confidential,” or adding a company name.
en.sotool.top/batch-watermark
5. Batch Delete Pages
Remove unwanted pages from multiple PDFs at once. Specify page ranges and the tool removes them across every file.
en.sotool.top/batch-delete-pages
6. Batch Add Page Numbers
Add page numbers to multiple PDFs with consistent formatting. Great for reports, manuals, and legal documents.
en.sotool.top/batch-page-numbers
7. Batch Encrypt
Password-protect multiple PDFs with the same open password. Useful for distributing sensitive documents securely.
en.sotool.top/batch-encrypt
8. Batch Decrypt
Remove passwords from multiple protected PDFs in one go.
en.sotool.top/batch-decrypt
9. Batch PDF to Image
Convert multiple PDFs to PNG or JPEG images at once.
en.sotool.top/batch-pdf-to-image
10. Batch Image to PDF
Convert multiple images or groups of images to separate PDFs.
en.sotool.top/batch-image-to-pdf
11. Batch Word to PDF
Convert multiple .docx files to PDF without needing Microsoft Word installed.
en.sotool.top/batch-word-to-pdf
12. Batch Excel to PDF
Convert multiple spreadsheets to PDF. Supports multi-sheet selection.
en.sotool.top/batch-excel-to-pdf
13. Batch Sign PDF
Apply the same electronic signature to multiple PDFs at once.
en.sotool.top/batch-sign-pdf
How it works

• Open any batch tool page

• Upload all your files at once (drag and drop or click to select)

• Configure the settings (angle, watermark text, DPI, password, etc.)

• Click process — each file is handled locally in your browser

• Download a single ZIP containing all results
Why browser-based batch processing matters
Traditional batch PDF tools send your files to a server, process them there, and send them back. That introduces:

• Privacy risk — your files leave your device

• Upload/download time — large batches take forever

• Server costs — which means paid subscriptions or file limits
Browser-based batch processing solves all of that. Files never leave your device, processing speed depends only on your computer, and there are no upload limits beyond what your browser can handle.
When you might need more
For everyday batch tasks (10–100 files), the browser tools are plenty. If you regularly process thousands of files, need OCR, or want complex workflow automation, a desktop tool like Wondershare PDFelement is worth considering.
Summary
Batch processing turns hours of repetitive work into a single click. Whether you need to merge, compress, watermark, rotate, or convert dozens of PDFs — all of these tools run free in your browser, no upload, no signup.
Try en.sotool.top/batch-merge and explore the other batch tools from the same site.
